title: harness.toml
description: Per-consumer fs-test-harness configuration. Consumed by both the Mac-side scripts and the Rust runner.

binary.description: Path to consumer's binary on the VM (relative to harness.toml or absolute). Substituted as {binary}.

host.description: Default user@host for SSH; setup-local.sh uses this as a prompt default.

packages.description: winget packages installed by setup-windows-vm.ps1. Each entry is either a bare PkgId string or a table { id = "PkgId", custom_args = "..." } where custom_args is forwarded to the underlying installer via winget's --override flag (e.g. ADDLOCAL=F.Core,F.Developer for WinFsp's developer-pack feature).

custom_args.description: Args forwarded to the underlying installer via winget --override. Use for non-default MSI/EXE features.

rust_toolchain.description: rustup channel-target-triple to set as default on the VM, e.g. "stable-aarch64-pc-windows-gnullvm".

env_prefix.description: PowerShell prefix prepended before the cargo invocation, e.g. setting LIBCLANG_PATH.

tools.description: Reference tool paths. Substituted into [ops] / [post_verify] templates as {tools.<name>}, e.g. {tools.fsck}.

ops.description: Op-name -> definition. Each value is either a bare command-template string (sugar for `{ host: "vm", command: <string>, expect_exit: 0 }`) or a table with explicit `host`, `command`, `expect_exit`, `when` fields. Reserved tokens in the command template: {binary}, {image}, {drive}, {path}, {from}, {to}, {content}, {extra}, {tools.<name>}, {scenario.<dotted.path>}, {step.<field>}, {x?}.
